heading: Amortization of goodwill and certain other intangibles
label: 197 Amortization of goodwill and certain other intangibles
notes: References in TextThe date of the enactment of this section, referred to in subsecs. (c)(1)(A) and (f)(9)(A), (F), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 103–66Aug. 10, 1993

L. 104–188, title I, § 1703110 Stat. 1877Aug. 10, 1993Aug. 20, 1996l“(1).—In generalExcept as otherwise provided in this subsection, the amendments made by this section [enacting this section and amending sections 167, 642, 848, 1016, 1060, 1245, and 1253 of this title] shall apply with respect to property acquired after the date of the enactment of this Act [].Aug. 10, 1993“(2)Election to have amendments apply to property acquired after .—july 25, 1991“(A).—In generalIf an election under this paragraph applies to the taxpayer—“(i)the amendments made by this section shall apply to property acquired by the taxpayer after ,July 25, 1991“(ii)subsection (c)(1)(A) of section 197 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (as added by this section) (and so much of subsection (f)(9)(A) of such section 197 as precedes clause (i) thereof) shall be applied with respect to the taxpayer by treating , as the date of the enactment of such section, andJuly 25, 1991“(iii)in applying subsection (f)(9) of such section, with respect to any property acquired by the taxpayer or a related person on or before the date of the enactment of this Act, only holding or use on , shall be taken into account.July 25, 1991“(B).—ElectionAn election under this paragraph shall be made at such time and in such manner as the Secretary of the Treasury or his delegate may prescribe. Such an election by any taxpayer, once made—“(i)may be revoked only with the consent of the Secretary, and“(ii)shall apply to the taxpayer making such election and any other taxpayer under common control with the taxpayer (within the meaning of subparagraphs (A) and (B) of section 41(f)(1) of such Code) at any time after , and on or before the date on which such election is made.August 2, 1993“(3)Elective binding contract exception.—“(A).—In generalThe amendments made by this section shall not apply to any acquisition of property by the taxpayer if—“(i)such acquisition is pursuant to a written binding contract in effect on the date of the enactment of this Act and at all times thereafter before such acquisition,“(ii)an election under paragraph (2) does not apply to the taxpayer, and“(iii)the taxpayer makes an election under this paragraph with respect to such contract.“(B).—ElectionAn election under this paragraph shall be made at such time and in such manner as the Secretary of the Treasury or his delegate shall prescribe. Such an election, once made—“(i)may be revoked only with the consent of the Secretary, and“(ii)shall apply to all property acquired pursuant to the contract with respect to which such election was made.”

children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: A taxpayer shall be entitled to an amortization deduction with respect to any amortizable section 197 intangible. The amount of such deduction shall be determined by amortizing the adjusted basis (for purposes of determining gain) of such intangible ratably over the 15-year period beginning with the month in which such intangible was acquired.

children.heading: No other depreciation or amortization deduction allowable
children.label: b No other depreciation or amortization deduction allowable
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (a), no depreciation or amortization deduction shall be allowable with respect to any amortizable section 197 intangible.

children.label: A
children.text: which is acquired by the taxpayer after the date of the enactment of this section, and

children.label: B
children.text: which is held in connection with the conduct of a trade or business or an activity described in section 212.

children.label: A
children.text: which is not described in subparagraph (D), (E), or (F) of subsection (d)(1), and

children.label: B
children.text: which is created by the taxpayer.

children.heading: Anti-churning rules
children.label: 3 Anti-churning rules
children.text: For exclusion of intangibles acquired in certain transactions, see subsection (f)(9).

children.label: A
children.text: goodwill,

children.label: B
children.text: going concern value,

children.label: i
children.text: workforce in place including its composition and terms and conditions (contractual or otherwise) of its employment,

children.label: ii
children.text: business books and records, operating systems, or any other information base (including lists or other information with respect to current or prospective customers),

children.label: iii
children.text: any patent, copyright, formula, process, design, pattern, knowhow, format, or other similar item,

children.label: iv
children.text: any customer-based intangible,

children.label: v
children.text: any supplier-based intangible, and

children.label: vi
children.text: any other similar item,

children.label: D
children.text: any license, permit, or other right granted by a governmental unit or an agency or instrumentality thereof,

children.label: E
children.text: any covenant not to compete (or other arrangement to the extent such arrangement has substantially the same effect as a covenant not to compete) entered into in connection with an acquisition (directly or indirectly) of an interest in a trade or business or substantial portion thereof, and

children.label: F
children.text: any franchise, trademark, or trade name.

children.label: i
children.text: composition of market,

children.label: ii
children.text: market share, and

children.label: iii
children.text: any other value resulting from future provision of goods or services pursuant to relationships (contractual or otherwise) in the ordinary course of business with customers.

children.heading: Special rule for financial institutions
children.label: B Special rule for financial institutions
children.text: In the case of a financial institution, the term “customer-based intangible” includes deposit base and similar items.

children.heading: Supplier-based intangible
children.label: 3 Supplier-based intangible
children.text: The term “supplier-based intangible” means any value resulting from future acquisitions of goods or services pursuant to relationships (contractual or otherwise) in the ordinary course of business with suppliers of goods or services to be used or sold by the taxpayer.

children.label: A
children.text: in a corporation, partnership, trust, or estate, or

children.label: B
children.text: under an existing futures contract, foreign currency contract, notional principal contract, or other similar financial contract.

children.heading: Land
children.label: 2 Land
children.text: Any interest in land.

children.label: i
children.text: computer software which is readily available for purchase by the general public, is subject to a nonexclusive license, and has not been substantially modified, and

children.label: ii
children.text: other computer software which is not acquired in a transaction (or series of related transactions) involving the acquisition of assets constituting a trade or business or substantial portion thereof.

children.heading: Computer software defined
children.label: B Computer software defined
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “computer software” means any program designed to cause a computer to perform a desired function. Such term shall not include any data base or similar item unless the data base or item is in the public domain and is incidental to the operation of otherwise qualifying computer software.

children.heading: Certain interests or rights acquired separately
children.label: 4 Certain interests or rights acquired separately
A
children.text: Any interest in a film, sound recording, video tape, book, or similar property.

children.label: B
children.text: Any right to receive tangible property or services under a contract or granted by a governmental unit or agency or instrumentality thereof.

children.label: C
children.text: Any interest in a patent or copyright.

children.label: i
children.text: has a fixed duration of less than 15 years, or

children.label: ii
children.text: is fixed as to amount and, without regard to this section, would be recoverable under a method similar to the unit-of-production method.

children.heading: Interests under leases and debt instruments
children.label: 5 Interests under leases and debt instruments
A
children.text: an existing lease of tangible property, or

children.label: B
children.text: except as provided in subsection (d)(2)(B), any existing indebtedness.

children.heading: Mortgage servicing
children.label: 6 Mortgage servicing
children.text: Any right to service indebtedness which is secured by residential real property unless such right is acquired in a transaction (or series of related transactions) involving the acquisition of assets (other than rights described in this paragraph) constituting a trade or business or substantial portion thereof.

children.heading: Certain transaction costs
children.label: 7 Certain transaction costs
children.text: Any fees for professional services, and any transaction costs, incurred by parties to a transaction with respect to which any portion of the gain or loss is not recognized under part III of subchapter C.

children.label: i
children.text: no loss shall be recognized by reason of such disposition (or such worthlessness), and

children.label: ii
children.text: appropriate adjustments to the adjusted bases of such retained intangibles shall be made for any loss not recognized under clause (i).

children.heading: Special rule for covenants not to compete
children.label: B Special rule for covenants not to compete
children.text: In the case of any section 197 intangible which is a covenant not to compete (or other arrangement) described in subsection (d)(1)(E), in no event shall such covenant or other arrangement be treated as disposed of (or becoming worthless) before the disposition of the entire interest described in such subsection in connection with which such covenant (or other arrangement) was entered into.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: C Special rule
children.text: All persons treated as a single taxpayer under section 41(f)(1) shall be so treated for purposes of this paragraph.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of any section 197 intangible transferred in a transaction described in subparagraph (B), the transferee shall be treated as the transferor for purposes of applying this section with respect to so much of the adjusted basis in the hands of the transferee as does not exceed the adjusted basis in the hands of the transferor.

children.label: i
children.text: any transaction described in section 332, 351, 361, 721, 731, 1031, or 1033, and

children.label: ii
children.text: any transaction between members of the same affiliated group during any taxable year for which a consolidated return is made by such group.

children.heading: Treatment of amounts paid pursuant to covenants not to compete, etc.
children.label: 3 Treatment of amounts paid pursuant to covenants not to compete, etc.
children.text: Any amount paid or incurred pursuant to a covenant or arrangement referred to in subsection (d)(1)(E) shall be treated as an amount chargeable to capital account.

children.heading: Franchise
children.label: A Franchise
children.text: The term “franchise” has the meaning given to such term by section 1253(b)(1).

children.heading: Treatment of renewals
children.label: B Treatment of renewals
children.text: Any renewal of a franchise, trademark, or trade name (or of a license, a permit, or other right referred to in subsection (d)(1)(D)) shall be treated as an acquisition. The preceding sentence shall only apply with respect to costs incurred in connection with such renewal.

children.heading: Certain amounts not taken into account
children.label: C Certain amounts not taken into account
children.text: Any amount to which section 1253(d)(1) applies shall not be taken into account under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount paid or incurred by the acquirer under the assumption reinsurance transaction, over

children.label: B
children.text: the amount required to be capitalized under section 848 in connection with such transaction.

children.heading: Treatment of certain subleases
children.label: 6 Treatment of certain subleases
children.text: For purposes of this section, a sublease shall be treated in the same manner as a lease of the underlying property involved.

children.heading: Treatment as depreciable
children.label: 7 Treatment as depreciable
children.text: For purposes of this chapter, any amortizable section 197 intangible shall be treated as property which is of a character subject to the allowance for depreciation provided in section 167.

children.heading: Treatment of certain increments in value
children.label: 8 Treatment of certain increments in value
children.text: This section shall not apply to any increment in value if, without regard to this section, such increment is properly taken into account in determining the cost of property which is not a section 197 intangible.

children.label: i
children.text: the intangible was held or used at any time on or after , and on or before such date of enactment by the taxpayer or a related person,July 25, 1991

children.label: ii
children.text: the intangible was acquired from a person who held such intangible at any time on or after , and on or before such date of enactment, and, as part of the transaction, the user of such intangible does not change, orJuly 25, 1991

children.label: iii
children.text: the taxpayer grants the right to use such intangible to a person (or a person related to such person) who held or used such intangible at any time on or after , and on or before such date of enactment.July 25, 1991

children.label: i
children.text: subparagraph (A) would not apply to an intangible acquired by the taxpayer but for the last sentence of subparagraph (C)(i), and

children.label: I
children.text: to recognize gain on the disposition of the intangible, and

children.label: II
children.text: to pay a tax on such gain which, when added to any other income tax on such gain under this title, equals such gain multiplied by the highest rate of income tax applicable to such person under this title,

children.label: I
children.text: the related person bears a relationship to such person specified in section 267(b) or section 707(b)(1), or

children.label: II
children.text: the related person and such person are engaged in trades or businesses under common control (within the meaning of subparagraphs (A) and (B) of section 41(f)(1)).

children.heading: Time for making determination
children.label: ii Time for making determination
children.text: A person shall be treated as related to another person if such relationship exists immediately before or immediately after the acquisition of the intangible involved.

children.heading: Acquisitions by reason of death
children.label: D Acquisitions by reason of death
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to the acquisition of any property by the taxpayer if the basis of the property in the hands of the taxpayer is determined under section 1014(a).

children.heading: Special rule for partnerships
children.label: E Special rule for partnerships
children.text: With respect to any increase in the basis of partnership property under section 732, 734, or 743, determinations under this paragraph shall be made at the partner level and each partner shall be treated as having owned and used such partner’s proportionate share of the partnership assets.

children.heading: Anti-abuse rules
children.label: F Anti-abuse rules
children.text: The term “amortizable section 197 intangible” does not include any section 197 intangible acquired in a transaction, one of the principal purposes of which is to avoid the requirement of subsection (c)(1) that the intangible be acquired after the date of the enactment of this section or to avoid the provisions of subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Tax-exempt use property subject to lease
children.label: 10 Tax-exempt use property subject to lease
children.text: In the case of any section 197 intangible which would be tax-exempt use property as defined in subsection (h) of section 168 if such section applied to such intangible, the amortization period under this section shall not be less than 125 percent of the lease term (within the meaning of section 168(i)(3)).

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: g Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be appropriate to carry out the purposes of this section, including such regulations as may be appropriate to prevent avoidance of the purposes of this section through related persons or otherwise.
